qualifying_org_definition: Any nonprofit or for-profit provider, public agency, or community service program site serving a useful purpose in health, social service, environmental protection, education, urban/rural development, welfare, recreation, public facilities, public safety, or child care. No MOU, pre-approved provider list, or county registration required for the general volunteerism lane.
verification_instrument: Form 805 (ABAWD Comparable Workfare Activity Form) — completed by the nonprofit/comparable workfare provider, not by the recipient or the county. The ABAWD self-arranges placement, the provider completes Form 805, and the recipient submits it to their SNAP eligibility specialist for case documentation.
hours_formula_detail: Monthly required hours = SNAP AU monthly allotment ÷ $7.25 federal minimum wage (truncated to whole number, no rounding). Example from PAMMS 3355: $292 ÷ $7.25 = 40 hours. Hours may not exceed 30/week. This means a typical ABAWD needs roughly 20–55 hours/month depending on their allotment, NOT the flat federal 80-hour default.
accepts_outofstate_detail: PAMMS 3355 imposes no geographic restriction on the comparable workfare provider. The policy states only that 'the ABAWD is responsible for arranging their own placement at a nonprofit or for-profit provider' — no language requires the organization to be Georgia-based or registered in Georgia. Under the federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying nonprofit may serve as the provider absent a state restriction. Operationally, a CA-based 501(c)(3) completing Form 805 should be accepted.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Georgia did not hold a statewide ABAWD waiver prior to OBBBA and has no county-level waivers as of 2026. All counties are time-limited ABAWD counties.
submission_deadline: At application and at recertification (renewal). Policy states participation must be verified 'timely to prevent an over-issuance of benefits' but no specific within-month deadline is stated. No monthly reporting required — verification is tied to certification periods.
notes:
Georgia is Tier 1 clean: the PAMMS policy manual (section 3355) explicitly includes 'volunteerism and/or community service' as qualifying work activity toward the ABAWD requirement, with no mandatory EDP, county assignment, MOU, or provider registration pre-step. The ABAWD self-arranges their placement at a nonprofit and the provider completes Form 805 for verification — this is a direct nonprofit-to-recipient certification path. Confidence is medium rather than high because the Comparable Workfare Guide PDF returned 403 Forbidden and the ABAWD FAQ page was also inaccessible, so the clean reading relies primarily on the PAMMS section 3355 policy text and press releases; no evidence of hoops was found but additional state documents could not be fully verified. ABAWD enforcement expanded under HR1/OBBBA effective November 1, 2025.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial research to try to refute the Tier 1 classification. Four independent searches and four direct PAMMS policy-manual fetches were performed. Key findings: (1) PAMMS 3355 was fetched directly and confirmed verbatim that volunteerism/community service qualifies, ABAWDs self-arrange placements at any qualifying nonprofit with no pre-approval list, and Form 805 is completed by the provider — no prestep language anywhere in the section. (2) PAMMS 3360 (E&T Participation Requirement) was fetched and confirmed that the Individual Employment Plan (IEP) requirement applies ONLY to SNAP Works (E&T program) enrollees, NOT to ABAWDs using the comparable workfare/volunteerism lane. Georgia explicitly operates two separate tracks: SNAP Works (requires IEP, orientation, assessment) and Comparable Workfare (no enrollment, no IEP, self-initiation). The IEP/EDP is not a prestep for Tended users. (3) PAMMS 3350 (Work Registration) was fetched and confirmed that work registration (Form 859 notice at application) is a standard eligibility-determination step, not a case manager assignment or EDP that must precede volunteer hours. (4) Searches for 'approved provider list,' 'approved site,' and 'approved organization' returned only PAMMS 3355, which contains no such list — self-arrangement is confirmed at any qualifying community service site. (5) The FY26 E&T State Plan PDF (dfcs.georgia.gov) was retrieved but is a binary-compressed PDF not readable via web fetch; the FNS state plan page at fns.usda.gov timed out. Neither refuted the Tier 1 reading. (6) Remote/virtual volunteering remains 'unknown' — no Georgia policy document found addressing this modality either way; this is a policy gap, not a restriction. Confidence raised from medium to HIGH: PAMMS 3355 and 3360 are the authoritative Georgia policy manual and were directly fetched, multiple independent sources (DFCS public page, press releases, PAMMS 3350) all converge on the same clean structure, and no evidence of a hidden prestep, MOU requirement, or approved-site list was found despite adversarial search targeting specifically those vectors.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) HOURS FORMULA — Georgia does NOT use the flat federal 80-hr default. PAMMS 3355 explicitly states the workfare formula: monthly hours = SNAP allotment ÷ $7.25 federal minimum wage, truncated to whole numbers, capped at 30 hrs/week. Example given: $292 ÷ $7.25 = 40 hours. monthly_hours_required set to 40 as a representative figure (actual varies by allotment). (2) OUT-OF-STATE NONPROFIT — Policy is silent; no geographic restriction on provider location found in PAMMS 3355 or any other accessed source. Federal default applies (any qualifying nonprofit). Operationally favorable for Tended as a CA-based 501(c)(3). (3) GEOGRAPHIC WAIVERS — None. Georgia had no pre-OBBBA statewide waiver and has no county-level waivers; all counties fully enforced. (4) SUBMISSION UX — Form 805 is a paper form submitted to the SNAP eligibility specialist at the county DFCS office. Reporting is tied to application and recertification, not monthly. No specific within-month deadline stated. (5) FORM FIELDS — Form 805 (extracted from PDF) requires: Organization Name, Organization Address, Organization Phone, Volunteer Supervisor Name (printed + signature), Client Name, Client ID #, Case #, hours completed, and month/year of participation. No EIN, DOB, recipient address, or notarization required.
